title: Library Materials / Instructional Materials (Local)
description: This policy governs the selection, use, and removal of library and instructional materials in the district's schools, including rules about what types of content are and are not permitted. It applies to all school libraries and classrooms, affecting students, teachers, librarians, and anyone involved in choosing or using educational materials. Recent updates reflecting state law now include clear definitions and specific prohibitions around pornographic or sexually explicit content, which matters to parents because it establishes the standards the district must follow to ensure school materials are age-appropriate for students.

timeline.summary: Adds definitions and prohibitions related to pornographic/sexually explicit library and instructional materials, referencing S.B. 12 (89th Texas Legislature). Prohibits possession, acquisition, and purchase of pornographic library materials. Defines 'pornographic material' broadly to include harmful material under Penal Code 43.24, indecent/profane content, sexually explicit material under Education Code 33.021, and materials that refer students to pornographic websites via links or QR codes.
changes_added: Definition of 'pornographic material' including: 'Harmful material' as defined by Section 43.24, Penal Code; 'indecent content' that portrays sexual or excretory organs or activities in a way that is patently offensive; 'profane content' including grossly offensive language considered a public nuisance; library material rated sexually explicit by selling vendor; 'sexually explicit material' as defined by Education Code 33.021; library material containing indecent or profane content; any material that is pervasively vulgar or educationally unsuitable as referenced in Board of Education v. Pico; any material that refers a person to an Internet website containing pornographic material including by use of a link or QR code as defined by Section 443.001 of the Texas Health and Safety Code.
The possession, acquisition, and purchase of pornographic library materials is prohibited.
timeline.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). Raised two specific concerns: (1) the addition of "unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments" as prohibited — terms he called undefined and ambiguous; (2) a new "prohibited terms" section banning "Latinx" and "minor attracted person," which he opposed. He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
